session_handoff.author: Marcus Daley
session_handoff.purpose: Fresh-session continuation prompt. Paste this into a new Claude Code command window. Claude Code will read it, invoke the brainstorming skill, and immediately begin designing Sub-project 1 without re-asking settled questions.

instructions_for_claude.step_1: Read this entire JSON before responding.
instructions_for_claude.step_2: Invoke the 'superpowers:brainstorming' skill immediately — do not respond before invoking it.
instructions_for_claude.step_3: After the skill loads, announce: 'Starting brainstorm for Sub-project 1 — Graph Compare Viewer. Context loaded from session handoff. Proceeding directly to approach proposals.'
instructions_for_claude.step_4: Skip all context-gathering questions — they are answered in locked_decisions below.
instructions_for_claude.step_5: Propose 2-3 implementation approaches for Sub-project 1, then present the design section by section per the brainstorming skill process.
instructions_for_claude.step_6: After Sub-project 1 design is approved and spec is written, invoke 'superpowers:writing-plans' to produce the implementation plan.
instructions_for_claude.step_7: Do NOT begin implementing until Marcus approves the spec.

developer_profile.name: Marcus Daley
developer_profile.background: Navy veteran (9 years leadership), Full Sail BS Online Game Development (Feb 6 2026), stay-at-home dad
developer_profile.focus: Portfolio-grade software — AAA studio practices applied universally. Quality over speed. Never quick-and-dirty.
developer_profile.brainstorm_standard: Full UI/UX standard for brainstorm artifacts is locked — see C:/ClaudeSkills/skills/brainstorm-artifact/templates/BRAINSTORM_ARTIFACT_UI_STANDARD.md
developer_profile.key_rule: Never suggest minimal or simplified designs. Always research the most advanced, sophisticated approach.

project_overview.name: Graphify Client Portal
project_overview.vision: A multi-tenant client portal delivered as self-contained per-client HTML bundles. Marcus runs a local generator script that takes a client's graph data + branding + brainstorm questions and outputs one standalone HTML file per client. Clients open it in their browser — they see their codebase graph, fill out a brainstorm questionnaire, and download a PRD. Marcus uses the same tool for his own projects. Each client file is fully isolated by design.
project_overview.hosting_model: Static bundle — no server, no auth infrastructure. Isolation is structural (each client has a different file). Marcus shares via email or link.
project_overview.design_tool_integration: Sigma.js preferred (open source, free, better performance than vis-network for large graphs). Evaluate at Sub-project 4.
project_overview.brainstorm_artifact_standard: All brainstorm components must meet the locked UI/UX standard (Part 1-12 of BRAINSTORM_ARTIFACT_UI_STANDARD.md). React 18 + lucide-react only. No external dependencies beyond browser APIs.

locked_decisions.portal_architecture: Hybrid static bundle generator — Option C locked. Python script runs locally, outputs one self-contained HTML per client.
locked_decisions.audience: Dual — Marcus uses internally AND clients interact directly. Clients see only their project (structural isolation, not auth).
locked_decisions.graph_viewer_foundation: Current graph.html (graphify-out/) is the base. Fixed version already has: convex hull hyperedge rendering, confidence-coded colors, edge hiding, Export PNG, INFERRED toggle, font scaling, null-guarded neighbors.
locked_decisions.sub_project_order: 1 (Compare Viewer) → 2 (Bundle Generator) → 3 (Brainstorm + PRD) → 4 (Sigma.js upgrade). Each sub-project ships working software independently.
locked_decisions.annotation_system: Floating sticky-note panel inside the browser view. Persists via localStorage. 'Copy All Notes' button. Client can annotate and copy back to Claude.
locked_decisions.prd_export: Brainstorm locked answers → PRD document. Downloads as markdown + PDF.
locked_decisions.no_cloud_costs: All four sub-projects must work with zero ongoing hosting or API costs. Open source only for graph rendering.

recent_commits: 1c3b14e — docs(graph): document mojibake root cause, isolated nodes, edge ID resolution in GRAPH_REPORT
fd60d3b — fix(graph): add explicit parentheses to edge-hide operator precedence
pending_tasks: Task 1: Browser verification of graph.html fixes (7 visual checks — requires Marcus to confirm in browser)

sub_project_1.name: Graph Compare Viewer
sub_project_1.priority: build first — foundation for all others
sub_project_1.description: Three-panel standalone HTML tool. Panel 1: fixed graph.html (current version with all 10 fixes). Panel 2: original graph.html (pre-fixes, from git history). Panel 3: GRAPH_REPORT.md rendered as styled HTML. Annotation panel (sticky notes + copy-to-clipboard). Toggle between panels. Diff-focused — shows 'what changed and why'.
open_design_questions: Panel layout: side-by-side iframes vs tab switcher vs split-pane with resizable dividers?
Annotation panel: always-visible sidebar vs floating overlay vs bottom drawer?
GRAPH_REPORT.md renderer: marked.js (CDN) vs custom markdown parser vs pre-rendered?
Original graph recovery: embed git blob directly in the compare tool vs run a script to extract it first?
acceptance_criteria: All three panels accessible without page reload
Annotations persist in localStorage across browser sessions
Copy All Notes produces clean text for pasting into Claude
Standalone HTML — no server required, opens directly from filesystem

sub_project_2.name: Client Bundle Generator
sub_project_2.description: Python script: takes graph.json + branding_config.json + brainstorm_questions.json → outputs client-[name].html. Self-contained bundle. Client sees only their project data.
sub_project_2.key_output: client-[name].html — fully self-contained, no external dependencies

sub_project_3.name: Brainstorm Artifact + PRD Export
sub_project_3.description: Full interactive React brainstorm component embedded in client bundle. Meets locked UI/UX standard (all 12 parts). PRD generation from locked answers. Downloads as markdown + PDF.
sub_project_3.theme: New theme needed — client-portal palette. To be designed during Sub-project 3 brainstorm.

sub_project_4.name: Design Tool Integration (Sigma.js)
sub_project_4.description: Replace or augment vis-network with Sigma.js for better large-graph performance (3227 nodes, 7871 edges). Integrate into both compare viewer and client bundle.
sub_project_4.rationale: vis-network struggles above ~2000 nodes. Sigma.js is WebGL-accelerated, open source, handles 100K+ nodes. Free, no API key required.

skills_to_invoke.session_start: superpowers:brainstorming — invoke FIRST before any response
skills_to_invoke.after_plan_written: superpowers:subagent-driven-development — execute plan task by task
skills_to_invoke.brainstorm_artifact: brainstorm-artifact — load before generating any brainstorm React component

first_message_after_skill_loads: Skip context questions — all locked decisions are in the JSON. Propose 2-3 layout approaches for the three-panel Graph Compare Viewer (Sub-project 1). Focus on: panel switching mechanism, annotation panel placement, and how to embed the original pre-fix graph without a server. Present trade-offs, give your recommendation, then wait for Marcus to choose.
